task_description: AzureFinOps skill end-to-end validation
reflection_q1: Should have run learn=true on all MCP tools before probing. Pricing tool parameter requirements caused 2 retries.
reflection_q2: Batch learn calls for all MCP tools in parallel at BUILD start to build parameter cache before EXECUTE.
reflection_q3: Predict from tool descriptions that service+sku is mandatory and use OData filter approach for non-VM resources.

task_description: Create weekly review template and first review
reflection_q1: Could have parallelized more data gathering in the first round
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have batched all reads in a single parallel call
reflection_q3: No additional capabilities needed for this straightforward file-creation task

task_description: Optimize CIDR doc format for LLM consumption
reflection_q1: Should have routed to BMAD Distillator skill first as the primary compression engine, then adapted for reference data
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have recognized distillation as the core task and used the existing distillator pipeline with reference-data extensions
reflection_q3: Should have used bmad-distillator for automated compression and Council for format debate (tables vs pure bullets)

task_description: Full project documentation refresh all modules
reflection_q1: Could have parallelized module README writes with background agents but sequential was fine since context was loaded
reflection_q2: Would have also updated docs/index.md to reference new ADR directory
reflection_q3: Could have used the docs-related skills more but managing-infra-skill was sufficient

task_description: Improve azure-reservations list-arm CLI UX output
reflection_q1: Should have extracted classify_resource_type as shared function from the start
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have started with mock rendering test before coding
reflection_q3: Council skill for debating UX layout choices

task_description: Document KQL project structure and context
reflection_q1: Should have detected missing BMAD workflow files before attempting skill invocation
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have a fast-path for document-project when no workflow.xml exists
reflection_q3: Research skill could have enriched the doc with KQL best practices from Microsoft docs

task_description: Document Hypera Loyalty Azure DevOps project context
reflection_q1: Should have checked for existing memory files before creating duplicates
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have enriched the existing overview rather than creating a new file
reflection_q3: No additional capabilities needed for pure documentation tasks

task_description: Document NetBackup implementation on AKS clusters
reflection_q1: Research agent was the right approach for 403d vendor docs
reflection_q2: Could have parallelized doc file creation with background agents for speed
reflection_q3: Could have used the Explore agent to check if similar docs existed in other repos

task_description: Python CLI for Azure quota increases with interactive selection
reflection_q1: Should have caught DRY violations during BUILD instead of needing simplify review
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would verify SDK import paths before writing files
reflection_q3: microsoft-docs skill was used but could have been invoked earlier during OBSERVE

task_description: Comprehensive ALZ documentation from multiple sources
reflection_q1: Could have started document skeleton during agent wait to save time
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have pre-structured the output file and filled sections as each agent completed rather than waiting for all
reflection_q3: Microsoft Learn MCP tool could have supplemented web fetches for more authoritative content

task_description: Plan complete Infracost integration for ALZ IaC
reflection_q1: Parallel research agents were well-calibrated — split by domain prevented overlap
reflection_q2: Could have checked Azure DevOps repo directly for actual pipeline YAML
reflection_q3: azure-finops-skill would have added actual cost baseline context for thresholds

task_description: Deep research Starlink cancellation for Mini users
reflection_q1: Should have anticipated Reddit domain block and used indirect search strategy from the start
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have parallelized WebFetch calls with initial searches and used Research skill for multi-agent parallel research
reflection_q3: Research skill would have enabled parallel agent research across all 7 angles simultaneously, saving significant time

task_description: Create NotebookLM from casey/just GitHub repo
reflection_q1: Could query notebook_list first to avoid duplicates
reflection_q2: Parallelized URL adds — efficient
reflection_q3: None missed; heavier thinking skills would be overkill

task_description: Create cmux docs NotebookLM with deep research
reflection_q1: Should have probed Quora URLs before calling notebook_add_url - 2 failed calls that could have been skipped.
reflection_q2: A templated topic-notebook flow taking {topic, user_urls[]} and auto-executing disambiguation->research->scrape->create->add->verify.
reflection_q3: Could have invoked Research skill or content-curator-skill explicitly instead of general-purpose agents.

task_description: Create complete justfile for ArgoCD project
reflection_q1: Assumed kubectl dry-run=client was offline; API discovery still fires. Verify runtime behavior before declaring recipe correct.
reflection_q2: Run one canonical recipe during EXECUTE to catch environmental mismatches, not only at VERIFY.
reflection_q3: Could have consulted managing-infra-skill for k8s justfile conventions; Standard tier single-file was fine without.

task_description: Build cmux studio plus 9 curated prompts
reflection_q1: Should stagger async media artifact generation first then sync ones to reduce polling surface.
reflection_q2: Use a templated full-studio helper that knows which artifacts are sync vs async and stages them.
reflection_q3: Could have used notebooklm-skill explicitly for built-in polling helpers.

task_description: Build notebooklm-create skill from session workflow
reflection_q1: Could have offered the skill-creator eval loop at the end — user skipped testing but the infrastructure is there.
reflection_q2: Smarter algorithm would pre-generate trigger eval queries in parallel while writing scripts.
reflection_q3: Could have invoked the skill-creator skill explicitly to delegate draft review.

task_description: Build NotebookLM notebook on iTerm2 docs
reflection_q1: Pattern matched prior notebook builds; no missteps.
reflection_q2: Fan out WebFetch + notebook_create concurrently; sequential constraint only applies to add_url.
reflection_q3: None missed — WebFetch + NotebookLM RPC were correct.

task_description: Build Azure Storage Account docs NotebookLM
reflection_q1: Should have probed HN URL with WebFetch before notebook_add_url to predict failure and skip the orphan placeholder.
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would parallelize canonical doc URL discovery via one structured search agent rather than sequential WebSearches.
reflection_q3: Should have used microsoft-docs skill for learn.microsoft.com searches instead of general WebSearch.

task_description: Recover cafehyna tfvars and fix justfile
reflection_q1: Should have inventoried sibling worktrees + global gitignore in OBSERVE before presenting options; user pushed back twice before I engaged proper research depth
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would run a completeness scan (worktrees/stash/reflog/global-ignore) before offering recovery options, grounding choices in what is actually recoverable
reflection_q3: Skill(simplify) post-edit, Skill(Research) explicit in OBSERVE rather than ad-hoc Task agents, TaskCreate for mechanical 12-ISC tracking

task_description: party-mode talk migrating secrets to Azure Key Vault
reflection_q1: I should have checked sprint-status.yaml for epic-number collision BEFORE writing the file — epic-6 was already reserved for AKS Networking. Renumbering took 12 edits that could have been zero.
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have run a pre-flight check: read sprint-status.yaml early, extract used epic numbers, choose the next free slot, THEN write the file. Also would have batched the 10+ rename edits using a single sed-style replace_all operation on the Write output instead of per-occurrence Edit calls.
reflection_q3: Could have invoked Skill(managing-infra-skill) more explicitly for Azure-native patterns — instead relied on MS Learn search directly. The skill exists for exactly this kind of K8s/TF/IaC architecture framing. Net-net MS Learn search gave authoritative WAF/MCSB control mappings which was the key value, so skipping it was defensible but suboptimal.

task_description: Build NotebookLM notebook on argocd-autopilot docs
reflection_q1: Could have probed readthedocs 403-block earlier and used github-docs proxy instead of retrying via WebFetch.
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have dispatched research agents FIRST (longest wall-clock), then done canonical URL discovery in parallel while agents ran.
reflection_q3: Could have used /simplify on the prompts.md output or Council skill for cross-persona chat prompt critique before adding.

task_description: Create obsidian vault notebook with all content
reflection_q1: Use Python for cross-file char counting instead of shell loops; shell portability issues in zsh caused wc/tr to fail inside loops
reflection_q2: Adaptive chunk limit (400k instead of 490k) would have prevented the sessions_chunk1 error at 485k chars near the limit
reflection_q3: Council or FirstPrinciples could have been used to optimize chunking strategy upfront

task_description: Generate all studio artifacts for obsidian vault notebook
reflection_q1: quiz and audio --language flag does not exist; check generate --help before assuming flags are universal
reflection_q2: fire all generations in a single batch rather than sequentially to save time; the timeouts were due to large notebook size not sequencing
reflection_q3: No additional skills needed; the notebooklm skill was sufficient and complete

task_description: Build azure finops NotebookLM notebook all studio
reflection_q1: Parallel agent dispatch + sequential URL adds was right. Memory-based handling of Quora/Medium anti-patterns prevented failures.
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would pre-fetch finops.org sitemap directly via WebFetch (simpler than spawning Explore agent for URL discovery).
reflection_q3: azure-finops-skill was listed but not invoked — skipped it; should have checked if it had domain-specific curation guidance

task_description: Workflow para identificar colunas em branco Azure FinOps
reflection_q1: Deveria ter verificado observacoes de sessao anterior antes de criar PRD do zero — a base ja existia
reflection_q2: Algoritmo mais inteligente detectaria contexto de melhoria de workflow existente e focaria nos gaps especificos
reflection_q3: FirstPrinciples foi a capability ideal — revelou suposicao limitante de segregacao de entidades vs. tratamento de nulos

task_description: Full rescan of Azure FinOps project documentation after finops-powerbi deletion
reflection_q1: The structural change detection (multi-part to monolith) worked well. Could have parallelized doc writes more aggressively from the start.
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have immediately diffed the git log against the cached project_classification to surface structural changes before any user interaction.
reflection_q3: No additional capabilities were needed for this workflow — it was documentation surgery, not code work.

task_description: Research best Power BI Dashboard for Azure Cost Management
reflection_q1: Could have opened browser to verify report page names exactly match before writing tile mappings
reflection_q2: Smart Narrative replacement for deprecated Q&A was a non-obvious finding that required research — validated against Microsoft Learn official deprecation notice
reflection_q3: Browser automation would have added visual verification of source report pages; azure-cost-management-app-skill could have been used for app structure verification

task_description: Document Power BI app using Microsoft Learn URL
reflection_q1: Deep-dive template designed for code files; external-reference docs need a different structure without file inventory sections
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have immediately classified this as external-reference deep-dive and skipped code-scan template sections
reflection_q3: microsoft-docs MCP was selected but unused since WebFetch returned full content — skip redundant capability selections when primary tool succeeds

task_description: Full rescan and update of FinOps project documentation
reflection_q1: Scan level selection question was already anticipated correctly; workflow routing was clean
reflection_q2: A smarter approach would auto-detect delta size from git log and pre-suggest Quick Scan without asking
reflection_q3: No additional skills needed; this was a targeted update, not a research or analysis task

task_description: Adapt dashboard plan to AzureCostManagementApp template report
reflection_q1: Should have scanned all report pages BEFORE writing the original plan — the mismatch between assumed custom pages and actual template pages required a full plan rewrite.
reflection_q2: A smarter algorithm would have included a prerequisite step: verify that the workspace contains expected report pages before committing to a tile mapping.
reflection_q3: Could have used the powerbi-skill to query available pages programmatically rather than relying on browser screenshot scanning.

task_description: rename dashboard update all references document project
reflection_q1: Hover-before-click pattern required for PBI Service list rows; bmad-document-project is interactive and not suited for surgical updates
reflection_q2: Direct surgical file edits plus browser rename is the right approach for targeted renames in this project
reflection_q3: No additional capabilities needed; Playwright + file edits covered it completely
